summary: Moved the ThrownKirbyStar portion of ft_08A4 from assembly into a new ftCommon C source/header, partially matching the file at 97.17% according to the PR report. The PR matched several small helpers and callbacks fully, added motion-variable and Kirby type/header support needed to express the code, and kept difficult routines behind inline-asm fallbacks for MUST_MATCH/WIP. No review discussion was present in the slice.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar.c and built it via obj_files.mk.
Removed a large block of ThrownKirbyStar assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s while leaving/exporting remaining labels such as ftCo_800BE494 and ftCo_800BE7C0 for cross-file calls.

Added ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar.h with prototypes for ftCo_800BD9E0, ftCo_800BDA50, ftCo_800BDA74, ftCo_800BDB58, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Anim, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_IASA, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Phys, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Coll, and ftCo_800BE494.
Implemented fully matched or reported-100% routines including ftCo_800BD9E0, ftCo_800BDA50, ftCo_800BDA74, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Anim, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_IASA, and ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Coll.
Partially matched larger routines ftCo_800BDB58 and ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Phys, with PR report scores of 93.55% and 98.64% respectively.
Added ftCommon motion variable storage for mv.co.thrownkirbystar, including thrower_gobj, velocity/scale fields, bitfields at fp+2358, saved scale, and ftCollisionBox coll_box.

Refactored Kirby fighter variables in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/types.h from absolute x222C-style field names to relative names such as x0, x4, x8_b0, victim_kind, x10, and x18, while preserving fp+ offset comments.
Updated ftKb_Init.c to use renamed Kirby fighter variable fields and typed ftKb_DatAttrs in PUSH_ATTRS.

Typed several Kirby SpecialN helper prototypes in ftKb_Init.h, including ftKb_SpecialN_800F5820, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898, ftKb_SpecialN_800F58AC, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A88, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5AC0, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F5AD8.
Changed ftCo_DatAttrs.xDC in src/melee/ft/types.h from int to float, enabling use as a scale value for the ThrownKirbyStar accessory path.
Adjusted tools/functions.sh header generation from item includes to ftCommon/forward.h plus placeholder.h.
smart_moves: Used a hybrid C/ASM strategy: readable C bodies are present for partially understood routines, while ASM fallbacks under MUST_MATCH/WIP preserve exact matching constraints where the compiler output was not yet solved.

Created a dedicated ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar source/header rather than leaving the work embedded in the generic ft_08A4 assembly file, improving locality and searchability for future ftCommon throw-state work.
Introduced typed forward declarations for ftKb_GObj, ftKb_Fighter, and ftKb_DatAttrs so common code can reference Kirby-specific thrower data without over-including full definitions.

Recovered semantic fields only where evidence was strong, such as ftKirby victim_kind and ftCommon thrownkirbystar.thrower_gobj/coll_box/scale, while retaining placeholder-style names for unclear fields and bitfields.
Used inline helpers to isolate compiler-sensitive expressions for scale and physics logic, such as inlineB0, inlineB1, and inlineA0.
Kept function names address-based for unknown helpers but used descriptive state callback names for the known animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks.
Updated function prototypes around Kirby SpecialN helpers to match actual call sites, reducing UNK_PARAMS use and making the new C code type-checkable.
decomp_lessons: Partial matches can still be high-value when they carve out a coherent state module and leave only the hard compiler-sensitive pieces behind ASM guards.
When decompiling cross-character common behavior, expect to add forward declarations and typed helper prototypes in both the common fighter and source-character headers.
Motion-variable unions should model the exact fp+ offsets and bit positions observed in assembly; this PR used fp+2340 through fp+2368 and fp+2358 bitfields for thrownkirbystar.
Do not over-name unknown motion-variable fields; keep x4/x8/x14/x18_b* style names with offset comments until behavior is proven.
Kirby copy/throw logic may need special FighterKind handling: this PR’s ftCo_800BD9E0 handles Kirby, Boy/Popo/Nana-related values, Sandbag, and maps Nana to Popo.
Type fixes in shared attribute structs can be required for a match; here ftCo_DatAttrs.xDC needed to be a float to support scale assignment.
Collision and physics callbacks often become clearer after extracting vector math helpers: this PR exposed lbVector_Mirror use and normal-based rebound logic in ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Coll.
Generated headers/tooling may need system-specific include defaults; this slice shows tools/functions.sh being adjusted away from item includes for ftCommon-style generation.
naming_conventions: Known ftCommon state callbacks use the pattern ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Anim, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_IASA, ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Phys, and ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Coll.
Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named helpers remain address-based, e.g. ftCo_800BD9E0, ftCo_800BDA50, ftCo_800BDA74, ftCo_800BDB58, ftCo_800BE494, and ftCo_800BE7C0.
Kirby-specific aliases use ftKb_* types in forward.h, such as ftKb_GObj, ftKb_Fighter, and ftKb_DatAttrs.
Kirby fighter variables moved from absolute-offset member names to relative struct member names while preserving fp+ offset comments; example: x2238 became victim_kind at fp+2238.
ftCommon motion vars use a lowercase union member name thrownkirbystar and retain placeholder field names for unknown behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted assembly into a new C file while preserving exact ASM fallback bodies with ASM, nofralloc, clang-format off, and #pragma peephole on around sensitive routines.
Used #if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP) to compile readable C in non-strict contexts and fall back to hand assembly for strict matching.
Retained literals with explicit symbols such as ftCo_804D8BD0, ftCo_804D8BE0, ftCo_804D8BE8, ftCo_804D8BF0, and ftCo_804D8BF4 to reproduce float/double constant references.
Promoted local assembly labels to globally callable symbols where the new C needed to call still-assembly routines, notably ftCo_800BE494 and ftCo_800BE7C0.
Used small inline functions to influence codegen for attribute access and physics math.
Matched simple empty callbacks directly as empty C functions for Anim and IASA.
Recovered sqrtf-style speed magnitude logic in C but retained ASM fallback for ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Phys, indicating that floating-point codegen remained sensitive.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence beyond the title was the matching report for ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar.c, showing file score 97.17% and per-function scores.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_800BDB58 or ftCo_ThrownKirbyStar_Phys.
Search for later renames of mv.co.thrownkirbystar.x4, x8, x14, x18_b0, and x18_b1.
Check whether ftCo_800BE494 and ftCo_800BE7C0 were later moved from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into C.
Look for follow-up work that names ftKirby fighter variable fields beyond victim_kind.
Search for later changes to tools/functions.sh header generation for non-item systems.
